notes.introduction: Preaching through 2 Corinthians 2:5-11, this sermon examines the subject of forgiveness within the church, using a disciplinary situation at Corinth as the framework for exploring what biblical forgiveness, comfort, and restoration look like among believers.
outline: I. The Conflict in Corinth (vv. 5-6): the unresolved tension over whether a repentant offender should be restored after church discipline
II. Paul's Counsel (vv. 7-10): a three-part instruction to forgive, comfort, and confirm love toward the offender, with supporting passages from Matthew 18 and Luke 17
III. Paul's Concern (v. 11): the warning that failure to forgive gives Satan an advantage, drawing on Ephesians 4 and 6 and other passages on spiritual warfare
themes: the character of god as a forgiving god
